title: asclepias grayi
intro: Asclepias grayi, also known as Gray's milkweed, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States. It is a member of the Asclepiadaceae family and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to induce vomiting and relieve fever. The plant contains cardenolides, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. Asclepias grayi can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its medicinal properties have been used in traditional medicine for centuries.
